subtasks.description: Query MongoDB to retrieve AWS contract documents with commitment levels, terms, commitment amounts, and contract dates
Query SQL data warehouse to retrieve AWS actual spend by month/period, broken down by service category to match contract commitment buckets

Reconcile contracted commitments with actual spend across time periods. Calculate overcommitment (commitments > spend) and underutilization (unused capacity). Classify each commitment segment as healthy, overcommitted, or underutilized. Generate prioritized recommendations for rebalancing.

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks are required: (1) mongo_query (small) retrieves contract metadata—straightforward document lookup; (2) sql_gen (mid) aggregates spend by period and service—requires multi-table join and time-based grouping; (3) cross_recon (frontier) reconciles two sources, calculates variance metrics, classifies commitment health, and prioritizes recommendations—high complexity with multi-signal decision logic. This is the minimum decomposition: we cannot merge the data sources into one subtask, and all analysis (comparison, classification, prioritization) belongs in a single cross_recon call, not separate tasks.

output.sql: SELECT cf.product AS service_category, DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date) AS spend_month, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS actual_spend, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.license_id) AS active_licenses, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) AS total_daily_active_seats, inv.contracted_amount, inv.contracted_amount - SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS variance, CASE WHEN inv.contracted_amount IS NULL THEN 'NO_CONTRACT' WHEN SUM(cf.amount_usd) > inv.contracted_amount THEN 'OVERCOMMITTED' WHEN SUM(cf.amount_usd) < inv.contracted_amount * 0.8 THEN 'UNDERUTILIZED' ELSE 'ON_TRACK' END AS commitment_status FROM consumption_facts cf LEFT JOIN ( SELECT vendor_name, SUM(amount_usd) AS contracted_amount, DATE_TRUNC('month', invoice_date) AS invoice_month FROM invoice_facts WHERE vendor_name ILIKE '%AWS%' OR vendor_name ILIKE '%Amazon Web Services%' GROUP BY vendor_name, DATE_TRUNC('month', invoice_date) ) inv ON DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date) = inv.invoice_month WHERE cf.publisher ILIKE '%AWS%' OR cf.publisher ILIKE '%Amazon%' GROUP BY cf.product, DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date), inv.contracted_amount ORDER BY spend_month DESC, actual_spend DESC
